Container.Name: caddy-https
Container.Overview: Caddy web server with automatic HTTPS via Cloudflare DNS challenge. Serves static files and/or acts as reverse proxy for multiple subdomains. NOTE: This template requires building the image locally from the Dockerfile. See the project repository for instructions.

Config.Name: HTTPS Port
Config.Description: HTTPS port exposed on the host (maps to container port 443)

Config.Name: HTTP/3 UDP Port
Config.Description: UDP port for HTTP/3 (QUIC). Set same value as HTTPS Port above. Safe to leave as-is if unsure.

Config.Name: HTTP Port
Config.Description: HTTP port (redirects to HTTPS, maps to container port 80)

Config.Name: Caddy Admin API Port
Config.Description: Caddy admin API port. Only expose if you need external access to caddy reload/config API. Leave unmapped for most setups.

Config.Name: Cloudflare API Token
Config.Description: Cloudflare API Token with Zone:DNS:Edit permission for your domain

Config.Name: Static HTML folder
Config.Description: Folder containing static HTML files. Mounted read-only for security.

Config.Name: Caddyfile
Config.Description: Path to your Caddyfile configuration on the host

Config.Name: Caddy Data (certificates)
Config.Description: Persistent storage for TLS certificates. Do not delete.

Config.Name: Caddy Config
Config.Description: Persistent storage for Caddy internal state
